steps_json: ["Give artemether-lumefantrine per weight band (see dosing card)", "Paracetamol for fever if temp > 38.5°C", "Oral rehydration. Return in 2 days or immediately if convulsions."]
["Give artemether-lumefantrine per weight band", "Paracetamol for fever", "Monitor for danger signs: convulsions, inability to drink, vomiting everything", "Return in 24 hours for follow-up"]
["Artemether-lumefantrine 4 tablets at 0, 8, 24, 36, 48, 60 hours", "Paracetamol 1g for fever", "Rest and fluids. Return if no improvement in 48 hours."]

["Do NOT give oral medication — aspiration risk", "Transfer to health facility IMMEDIATELY", "If convulsing: place in recovery position, protect airway", "Give rectal artesunate suppository if available and transfer will take > 6 hours"]
["Refer to hospital immediately — IV artesunate required", "Monitor consciousness and breathing during transport", "Do not give oral antimalarials"]
["Amoxicillin 40mg/kg/day in 2 doses for 5 days", "Paracetamol for fever", "Encourage fluids and breastfeeding", "Return immediately if breathing worsens, child cannot drink, or lips turn blue"]
["Amoxicillin syrup per weight — see dosing card", "Keep warm, continue breastfeeding", "Return in 2 days or immediately if rapid breathing worsens"]

["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Give first dose of amoxicillin before transfer if available", "Keep child upright during transport", "Count breaths — severe if > 50/min (infants) or > 40/min (children)"]

["Oral rehydration salts (ORS) — give 50-100mL after each loose stool", "Continue breastfeeding", "Zinc 20mg daily for 10 days", "Return if child cannot drink, has blood in stool, or condition worsens"]

["ORS 75mL/kg over 4 hours", "Zinc 20mg daily for 10 days", "Reassess after 4 hours — if improved, continue ORS at home", "Return immediately if sunken eyes worsen, skin pinch returns slowly, child cannot drink"]
["Refer to health facility IMMEDIATELY — IV fluids needed", "Give ORS sips during transport if child can swallow", "Signs of severe dehydration: sunken eyes, skin pinch very slow, lethargic"]

["Do NOT give food or water — aspiration risk", "Transfer to nearest OTP/ITFC immediately", "If unconscious: recovery position, monitor breathing", "MUAC < 11.5cm or visible wasting with edema = SAM"]
["Emergency referral to ITFC", "Continue breastfeeding if conscious", "Do NOT give F-75 or F-100 formula outside facility"]

["Enroll in OTP (Outpatient Therapeutic Program)", "Provide RUTF (Ready-to-Use Therapeutic Food) per weight", "MUAC 11.5-12.5cm = MAM — monitor weekly", "Return immediately if MUAC drops below 11.5cm"]
["Vitamin A 200,000 IU orally today and tomorrow", "Paracetamol for fever if temp > 38.5°C", "Fluids and rest", "Return if rash spreads to eyes, breathing worsens, or child cannot drink"]
["Vitamin A 200,000 IU today and tomorrow", "Paracetamol for fever", "Check eyes daily — treat conjunctivitis with tetracycline eye ointment if present", "Return in 2 days for follow-up"]
["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Give Vitamin A 200,000 IU before transfer", "Complications: pneumonia, encephalitis, severe eye involvement, severe malnutrition"]

["Refer to health facility for sputum test", "Symptoms: cough > 2 weeks, night sweats, weight loss, blood in sputum", "Do NOT start TB treatment without lab confirmation", "Isolate patient from others during transport"]
["Refer to health facility for evaluation", "Child TB signs: failure to thrive, persistent cough, contact with known TB case", "Mantoux test or Xpert MTB/RIF required for diagnosis"]

["Refer for HIV testing and counseling", "Do NOT disclose HIV status without consent", "If on ART: check adherence and refer to ART clinic", "Opportunistic infections (thrush, PCP, TB) require hospital assessment"]

["Emergency referral IMMEDIATELY — IV antibiotics required", "Signs: not feeding, convulsions, fast/slow breathing, hypothermia, bulging fontanelle", "Keep warm during transport — wrap in cloth, skin-to-skin if possible", "Do NOT give any oral medication"]

["Refer to hospital for phototherapy assessment", "Jaundice visible before 24 hours of age = always emergency", "Jaundice with refusal to feed, lethargy, or high-pitched cry = emergency", "Ensure adequate feeding during transport"]

["Refer to health facility for bilirubin check", "Physiological jaundice appears day 2-3, resolves by day 14", "Ensure frequent breastfeeding (8-12 times/day)", "Return immediately if baby becomes lethargic or stops feeding"]

["Paracetamol for fever if temp > 38.5°C", "Test for malaria with RDT if available", "Ensure adequate fluids and rest", "Return in 2 days or immediately if: convulsions, stiff neck, rash, breathing difficulty"]
["Malaria RDT immediately", "Paracetamol for fever", "If RDT positive: treat per malaria protocol", "If RDT negative: seek source — ear, throat, urine", "Return in 24 hours"]
["Not pneumonia — no antibiotics needed", "Paracetamol for fever and discomfort", "Saline nasal drops for congestion", "Encourage fluids", "Return if breathing becomes fast or child cannot drink"]

["Clean wound with clean water and soap", "Apply topical antiseptic (gentian violet or chlorhexidine)", "Amoxicillin if spreading or signs of cellulitis", "Return if red streaks appear or fever develops"]
["Clean with soap and water, apply antiseptic", "Amoxicillin 500mg 3x/day for 5 days if infected", "Lance abscess if fluctuant and pointing", "Return if fever, red streaks, or spreading redness"]
["Clean eye with clean cloth and water", "Tetracycline eye ointment 3x/day for 7 days", "Return if vision blurred, eye swollen shut, or no improvement in 3 days"]

["Control bleeding with firm pressure for 10 minutes", "Clean wound thoroughly with soap and water", "Apply antiseptic", "Check tetanus immunization status — give TT if not up to date", "Return if signs of infection appear (heat, pus, red streaks)"]

["Apply direct pressure to control bleeding — do not remove", "If possible arterial bleeding: apply tourniquet proximal to wound", "Immobilize fractures with available splint material", "Transfer to hospital IMMEDIATELY"]

["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Danger signs: heavy bleeding, severe headache with visual disturbance, convulsions, no fetal movement", "Place in left lateral position during transport", "Do NOT delay for any reason"]
